output:
CVE-2015-10025 is a vulnerability found in the luelista miniConf platform, affecting versions up to 1.7.6. This flaw is classified as a Denial of Service (DoS) vulnerability that is caused by an issue in the URL Scanning component, specifically in the miniConf/MessageView.cs file. The type of vulnerability is associated with CWE-404, which involves improper resource management where a resource is not correctly released for reuse.

The severity of this vulnerability is rated as HIGH, indicating a significant potential impact if exploited. It affects versions up to 1.7.6 of miniConf, as identified by the known software vulnerability pattern. To mitigate this risk, it is recommended to upgrade to version 1.7.7 or 1.8.0 where the issue has been addressed with patch c06c2e5116c306e4e1bc79779f0eda2d1182f655. This CVE is also associated with the MITRE ATT&CK technique T1499, related to DoS attacks. Implementing mitigation technique M1037 could further enhance security measures.
